pattern: Elements sharing a limit form an independent wave: each wave resets the active count to zero once its threshold is crossed, so you may bank the highest-value picks up to that limit many times.
hints: Once the active count reaches some value x, everything with limit at most x dies, which resets how many are active for the smaller-limit choices.
Group the elements by their limit value; within a group with limit L you can perform at most L activations before the count reaches L and wipes that group.
For each distinct limit L, sort its elements by value and add the L largest (or all of them if there are fewer than L); sum these across all groups.

edge_inputs.description: mixed limits, drop the smallest of the over-full group
all limit one, only the single best can be taken
a limit-two group must shed its weakest element
group size equals its limit, take all of it
limit-one element dies before a duplicate-limit one helps

stress.description: many elements with limits spread across the range so several waves fill or overflow
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    value = [rng.randrange(1, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    limit = [rng.randrange(1, n + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [value, limit]

stress.description: limits clustered into a few values to create heavily overflowing waves
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    value = [rng.randrange(1, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    choices = [1, 2, 3, n // 2 if n >= 2 else 1, n]
    limit = [rng.choice(choices) for _ in range(n)]
    return [value, limit]
